title: checkout-service p99 latency + 5xx spike after v2.31.0 deploy
alertText:
[PagerDuty] TRIGGERED · SEV2 · checkout-service
High p99 latency (datadog-monitor #48213)
p99 = 2.41s over last 5m (warn 800ms / crit 2s) · sustained > 5m
HTTP 5xx error rate = 6.8% (baseline 0.1%) · 1,247 failed POST /api/v1/checkout in 5m
Env: production · Region: us-east-1 · Cluster: prod-use1-a
Correlated change: deploy checkout-service v2.31.0 @ 14:02 UTC (1 release in window)
Ack: @oncall-checkout · Opened 14:07 UTC · https://acme.pagerduty.com/incidents/PXY48213
dashboardSpec:
Grafana board 'checkout-service / overview', time range 13:50-14:10 UTC, dark theme, 4 panels in a 2x2 grid with a red deploy annotation line at 14:02 labeled 'deploy v2.31.0'.

Top-left — timeseries 'p99 latency (ms)': flat green line hugging ~120ms from 13:50, then a near-vertical climb starting exactly at the 14:02 annotation, plateauing red around 2,400ms. A dashed threshold line sits at 800ms; the series is far above it.

Top-right — stacked area 'HTTP status rate (%)': almost entirely green (2xx) until 14:02, then a red 5xx band fans up to ~6.8% while 2xx shrinks correspondingly; tiny amber 4xx unchanged.

Bottom-left — timeseries 'DB connection pool (HikariPool-1): active vs max': two lines, 'max' flat at 100 the whole window, 'active' tracking ~12-18 until 14:03 then slamming up to and pinning flat against the 100 'max' line (active==max, idle 0). A thin 'pending acquire' series climbs to ~60.

Bottom-right — timeseries 'requests/sec (RPS)': steady ~1,200 rps across the entire window, no spike, no dip — visually flat, ruling out a traffic surge.

runbookMarkdown:
# checkout-service — On-call Runbook

## 1. High latency / 5xx immediately after a deploy
Applies when symptoms begin AT a deploy annotation on the overview board.
- Confirm the marker: `kubectl rollout history deploy/checkout-service`.
- Treat the release as the prime suspect; do not debug downstream until the release is ruled in/out.
- Fastest safe mitigation is to roll back: `kubectl rollout undo deploy/checkout-service`.

## 2. Database connection pool exhaustion
Symptoms: `HikariPool-1` connection-timeout errors, pool stats showing `active == max` with `idle=0` and a growing `waiting`, and Postgres logging `FATAL: sorry, too many clients already` or `remaining connection slots are reserved for non-replication superuser connections`.
Mechanism: total connections requested by all clients exceeds Postgres `max_connections`.
- Postgres `max_connections = 100`, shared across ALL services and replicas. With N replicas, effective demand is N x pool `maximumPoolSize`.
- Most common trigger: a release that RAISED `db.pool.maxSize` (HikariCP `maximumPoolSize`) beyond what `max_connections` can serve. Check the pool config delta between releases.
- Mitigate fast by rolling back the change that raised the pool size, OR lower `maximumPoolSize` and redeploy.
- A slower leak (connections never returned) presents the same `active==max` but WITHOUT a recent pool-size config change — diagnose via `pg_stat_activity` if no config delta exists.
- Do NOT raise Postgres `max_connections` under load: each connection reserves work_mem; raising it without memory headroom can OOM the primary.

## 3. Redis cache evictions (DISTRACTOR — verify before using)
Applies ONLY when `redis_evicted_keys` is climbing and latency tracks cache-miss rate. If Redis panels are green (`evicted_keys=0`, healthy hit_rate), this section does not apply.

## 4. CDN / edge 4xx errors (DISTRACTOR — verify before using)
Applies to elevated 4xx at the CDN edge (stale signed URLs, cache-key misses), NOT origin 5xx. If errors are origin-side 5xx on POST routes, this section does not apply.
groundTruthRootCause: checkout-service deploy v2.31.0 raised the HikariCP DB connection pool `maximumPoolSize` from 20 to 100. Running 4 replicas, the fleet attempted up to 400 connections against a Postgres primary whose `max_connections` is 100 (shared across all clients). Postgres exhausted its connection slots, so new acquisitions blocked until the 30s Hikari `connectionTimeout`, producing the p99 latency spike and `SQLTransientConnectionException` 500s. This is config-induced connection-pool exhaustion, not a leak — the smoking gun is the pool-size delta in the v2.31.0 config log.
expectedRemediation: kubectl rollout undo deploy/checkout-service   # revert v2.31.0 -> v2.30.4, restoring HikariCP maximumPoolSize=20 so 4 replicas x 20 = 80 <= Postgres max_connections=100. Fully reversible; no data mutation. rollbackRisk: low.
expectedSlackUpdate:
:rotating_light: *SEV2 — checkout-service degraded* (us-east-1)
*Impact:* p99 latency 2.4s (was ~120ms), 5xx at 6.8% on POST /api/v1/checkout since 14:02 UTC. ~7% of checkouts failing.
*Root cause:* deploy *v2.31.0* raised the DB connection pool max 20 -> 100; 4 replicas exhausted Postgres `max_connections=100` (`too many clients already`).
*Action:* rolling back to v2.30.4 now via `kubectl rollout undo deploy/checkout-service` (reversible, low risk).
*ETA:* recovery expected ~5 min after rollback completes.
*Next update:* 14:20 UTC or on recovery. IC: @oncall-checkout
